operation: Start by creating a rectangular base of 40x30x12 mm on the XY plane. On the top face (positive Z), establish a workplane and place a cylinder with an 8 mm radius and 18 mm height centered on that face; since the cylinder is centered on the workplane by default, it extends both above and into the base. Next, select the bottom face (negative Z), create a new workplane, and apply a translation of 15 mm in X and 10 mm in Y. On this offset point, drill a countersink hole with a 5.5 mm shank diameter, an 8 mm countersink diameter, a 9-degree angle, and a depth of 4 mm.
geometry: The model features a rectangular slab as its primary body. A large cylindrical boss is situated at the center of the top surface, while a smaller, countersunk hole is positioned asymmetrically near one of the corners on the bottom surface. The resulting part has different functional features on its opposing flat faces.
function: This part functions as a mounting or locating base. The central cylindrical feature can serve as a pivot or alignment post, while the offset countersunk hole provides a recessed fastening point for securing the block to a mating assembly with a flush-mounted screw.
